canonical_truth.summary: Familiar terrain gives way during routine work; the close call comes from ground made known through repeated use, not from venturing into the unknown.
canonical_truth.location: branch-specific: east channel edge / shade corridor midpoint / stream northeast curve
canonical_truth.concrete_outcome: Each branch recovers without serious injury; each near-miss is caused by terrain altered by the stag's passage the day before or by accumulated drainage pressure.

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: the ground that has held every previous time does not hold; the familiar route has become unsafe at a specific point

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: World Day for Safety and Health at Work (April 28) — the physical cost that daily labor extracts, invisible until the moment it nearly isn't
authorial_note.translation_logic: Workers' Memorial Day marks harm that comes from ordinary work in ordinary places; each branch's hazard is native to the route it built through repeated use, not from straying into danger
authorial_note.restraint_reason: The event enters through terrain failure, not announcement; the world-day theme is embodied in the hazard's cause and location, not named
